question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: AddErrorDetails()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger unbounded heap growth by repeatedly calling endpoints that invoke AddErrorDetails() with arbitrarily large key/description strings, causing the server process to exhaust available memory and crash, denying service to all users of the Rosetta node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: AddErrorDetails()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes AddErrorDetails() to be called in a tight loop with a deeply nested chain of cloned Details maps, where each call to maps.Copy() copies an ever-growing map, leading to O(n^2) memory allocation and CPU exhaustion that crashes the process?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that newError() appends every error to the global Errors slice at package initialization time, and if any code path allows dynamic error registration, cause the Errors slice to grow without bound, exhausting memory and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Variable: Errors] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the global Errors slice (initialized with make([]*types.Error, 0)) is read concurrently without synchronization while being iterated in the /network/options response, causing a data race that corrupts the slice header and panics the server?
